methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ClearSpace, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Luc Piguet, is valued at $400M post-money following $36M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the space and aerospace sector, including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $400M last post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, implies $800M. Peer-median analysis across 3 same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M) yields a median valuation of $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data. The peer range spans $400M to $2.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $680M to $1.5B, with a median of $1.1B. The current $400M valuation sits at the lower bound of peer comparables, suggesting either a discount to similar-stage peers or a reflection of earlier traction. Key uncertainties include ClearSpace's revenue trajectory, technical execution risk on core operations, and valuation volatility in the early-stage space sector.
